text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived high in a towering oak tree. Every morning, he would gaze down at the bustling forest below, pondering the days' events. One day, a little squirrel asked him why he always seemed so contemplative.

"The great owl," the owl began thoughtfully, "ever since I first saw that girl in the meadow, I can't stop wondering who they'd choose as their lifetime companion."

The squirrel chuckled. "Why owls? Are you longing for owl mates?"

The owl shook his head. "No. It's her eyes. They sparkle like starlight during the full moon. Every time I see them..."

The squirrel listened intently, hanging onto every word.

"...I can't help wondering what kind of bird might appreciate eyes so captivating."

They laughed together, a bit of owl wisdom dribbled out between chuckles.
